# jfk-speech-10s.* — license & provenance

## Audio source

`samples/jfk.wav` from the [whisper.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp)
project, ggerganov/whisper.cpp on GitHub. Distributed under the MIT
license alongside the whisper.cpp source. The repo's license text:
<https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp/blob/master/LICENSE>.

Upstream file SHA-256 (verified at fixture-build time, 2026-05-20):

```
59dfb9a4acb36fe2a2affc14bacbee2920ff435cb13cc314a08c13f66ba7860e
```

## Audio content

John F. Kennedy, inaugural address, delivered 20 January 1961, Washington
DC. The clip contains the famous passage:

> "And so, my fellow Americans, ask not what your country can do for you,
> ask what you can do for your country."

As a work of the US federal government, this recording is **public
domain** under 17 USC § 105. Public-domain audio combined with the MIT-
licensed distribution channel imposes no additional restrictions on
redistribution.

## Files in this fixture set

| File | Purpose | License |
|---|---|---|
| `jfk-speech-10s.mp4` | 720×1280 black video + JFK audio muxed for renderer testing | Audio: public domain · MP4 container & encoded streams: MIT (matches repo) |
| `jfk-speech-10s.transcript.json` | Hand-aligned word-timed transcript | Original to this repo · MIT |
| `jfk-speech-10s.LICENSE.md` | This file | MIT |

The original `jfk.wav` 16 kHz mono PCM file is **not committed** — only
the MP4 muxed copy is. The MP4's audio stream is AAC-encoded at 128 kbps;
the original PCM can be recovered with `ffmpeg -i jfk-speech-10s.mp4 -ac 1 -ar 16000 jfk.wav`.

## Why this fixture

Phase B (v0.3.0) integration tests need a known speech sample for:
- R4c (Whisper re-ASR validation — cut a word, prove ASR no longer
  reports it in the output)
- R4f (frame-level idempotency under `CF_RENDER_DETERMINISTIC=1`)

JFK was chosen because it's clean, well-recorded, in the public domain,
and already shipped with whisper.cpp — minimizing license questions and
giving us a fixture whose word boundaries are stable across re-ASR
re-runs against any modern Whisper model.

## Transcript timing accuracy

Hand-aligned using `ffmpeg silencedetect` phrase boundaries as anchors
plus linear word distribution within each phrase. Phrase boundaries are
tight (within silencedetect's ~10 ms resolution). Within-phrase word
boundaries are approximate (±50 ms typical, ±100 ms worst case). Word
timings are intentionally NOT generated by an ASR engine to avoid
self-referential bias when R4c uses Whisper to verify cuts.

## Reproducibility

This fixture is bit-reproducible from upstream. To regenerate from a
clean checkout:

```bash
# 1. Fetch upstream jfk.wav (16 kHz mono PCM, ~344 KB)
curl -L -o /tmp/jfk.wav \
  https://github.com/ggerganov/whisper.cpp/raw/master/samples/jfk.wav

# 2. Verify upstream integrity (single source of truth for the audio bytes)
echo "59dfb9a4acb36fe2a2affc14bacbee2920ff435cb13cc314a08c13f66ba7860e  /tmp/jfk.wav" \
  | sha256sum -c

# 3. Mux with synthetic video into MP4 (the committed artifact)
DUR=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 /tmp/jfk.wav)
ffmpeg -y -hide_banner -loglevel error \
  -f lavfi -i "color=c=black:s=720x1280:r=30:d=${DUR}" \
  -i /tmp/jfk.wav \
  -c:v libx264 -tune stillimage -preset ultrafast -crf 30 -pix_fmt yuv420p \
  -c:a aac -b:a 128k -shortest \
  tests/fixtures/jfk-speech-10s.mp4
```

### SHA stability notes

- **Upstream `jfk.wav` SHA-256**: stable across re-fetches, verifiable as above.
- **Committed `jfk-speech-10s.mp4` SHA-256**: **varies by ffmpeg version**
  (libx264 + AAC + mp4 muxer emit version-tagged metadata and have
  preset-dependent encode output). Do not assert on the file-level SHA.
- **Committed `jfk-speech-10s.mp4` audio-stream MD5**: stable across
  ffmpeg re-encodes of the same input because libfdk-aac is not used (the
  AAC encoder built into ffmpeg is deterministic given identical input).
  Recover with:

  ```bash
  ffmpeg -hide_banner -loglevel error -i tests/fixtures/jfk-speech-10s.mp4 \
    -map 0:a -f md5 -
  ```

  Recorded at first commit: `MD5=52e31d517ae613231fb7dbb3cf60b8a5`.
  Subsequent regenerations using the same upstream `jfk.wav` and the
  exact mux command above should match. Mismatch indicates either a
  different upstream `jfk.wav` or a different ffmpeg build of the AAC
  encoder.
